data.title: JSW Energy Shares Rise 1.74% Amid High Trading Volumes in Today's Session
data.description:
Disclaimer This is an AI-assisted live blog with updates sourced from multiple news outlets and agencies Disclaimer

Shares of JSW Energy experienced a notable surge during today's trading session, climbing by 1.74% to reach Rs 495.90. This increase was accompanied by high trading volumes, indicating strong investor interest and activity in the stock.

The increased volume and price movement have drawn attention from market participants. JSW Energy's position is further highlighted by its inclusion in key indices such as the NIFTY NEXT 50 and NIFTY 100, underscoring its significance in the broader market.

Financial Performance


Recent financial data provides insights into JSW Energy's performance.

Quarterly Performance (Consolidated):

  • March 2025: Revenue stood at Rs 3,189.39 Cr, a notable increase from Rs 2,438.88 Cr in December 2024. Net profit was reported at Rs 409.14 Cr, compared to Rs 152.28 Cr in the previous quarter. The Earnings Per Share (EPS) was Rs 2.34.
  • September 2024: Revenue reached Rs 3,237.66 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 868.34 Cr and an EPS of Rs 4.90.
  • June 2024: Revenue was Rs 2,879.46 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 530.37 Cr and an EPS of Rs 3.00.
  • March 2024: Revenue was Rs 2,755.87 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 338.42 Cr and an EPS of Rs 2.14.

Annual Performance (Consolidated):
  • March 2024: Revenue was Rs 11,485.91 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 1,708.14 Cr and an EPS of Rs 10.50.
  • March 2023: Revenue was Rs 10,331.81 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 1,460.83 Cr and an EPS of Rs 9.01.
  • March 2022: Revenue was Rs 8,167.15 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 1,734.94 Cr and an EPS of Rs 10.52.
  • March 2021: Revenue was Rs 6,922.20 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 805.53 Cr and an EPS of Rs 4.84.
  • March 2020: Revenue was Rs 8,272.71 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 1,053.14 Cr and an EPS of Rs 6.70.

Key Financial Ratios


The financial ratios for JSW Energy provide additional context to the company’s financial health.
  • Debt to Equity: Increased to 1.50 in March 2024 from 0.71 in March 2020.
  • Return on Equity (ROE): 8.26% in March 2024.
  • Basic EPS: Rs 10.50 in March 2024.

The company’s performance in key indices such as NIFTY NEXT 50 and NIFTY 100 reflects its important standing in the market. The current price movement and volume surge may reflect growing investor confidence in JSW Energy's financial outlook.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Premier Energies Shares Rise 2.15% in Today's Trading Session
data.description:
Disclaimer This is an AI-assisted live blog with updates sourced from multiple news outlets and agencies Disclaimer

Premier Energies' stock is trading higher by 2.15% at Rs 1,057.95 in today's session, according to the latest market data. The price movement is based on IW data. The stock is part of the NIFTY MIDCAP 150 index.

Financial Performance


A review of Premier Energies' financial performance reveals the following trends:

Quarterly Performance:

  • Revenue: Revenue has shown growth over the last five quarters. The revenue for Dec 2023 was Rs 712.48 Cr, which increased to Rs 1,713.32 Cr by Dec 2024.
  • Net Profit: The net profit has also increased significantly, from Rs 43.30 Cr in Dec 2023 to Rs 255.08 Cr in Dec 2024.
  • EPS: Earnings per share have risen from Rs 1.29 in Dec 2023 to Rs 5.66 in Dec 2024.

Annual Performance:
  • Revenue: The company's revenue increased from Rs 1,428.53 Cr in 2023 to Rs 3,143.79 Cr in 2024.
  • Net Profit: Premier Energies turned profitable, with net profit increasing from a loss of Rs -14.56 Cr in 2023 to a profit of Rs 230.04 Cr in 2024.
  • EPS: Earnings per share also improved, from Rs -0.38 in 2023 to Rs 6.93 in 2024.
  • BVPS: Book Value per Share increased from Rs 9.16 in 2023 to Rs 18.10 in 2024.
  • ROE: Return on Equity significantly improved from -5.30 in 2023 to 48.50 in 2024.
  • Debt to Equity: The Debt to Equity ratio has slightly decreased from 3.16 in 2023 to 2.92 in 2024.

Cash Flow


Analyzing the cash flow data:
  • Net Cash Flow: The company's Net Cash Flow has improved from Rs -15 Cr in March 2023 to Rs 192 Cr in March 2024.

Balance Sheet


The balance sheet shows the following:
  • Total Assets: Total Assets have increased from Rs 2,110 Cr in March 2023 to Rs 3,554 Cr in March 2024.
  • Total Liabilities: Total Liabilities have increased from Rs 2,110 Cr in March 2023 to Rs 3,554 Cr in March 2024.

Financial Ratios


Here’s a look at some key financial ratios:
  • Basic EPS: Increased from Rs -0.38 in Mar 2023 to Rs 6.93 in Mar 2024.
  • Diluted EPS: Increased from Rs -0.38 in Mar 2023 to Rs 5.48 in Mar 2024.
  • Return on Networth / Equity: Improved significantly from -5.30% to 48.50%.
  • Debt to Equity: Decreased from 3.16 to 2.92.

According to a Moneycontrol analysis on 2025-05-09, the sentiment for the stock is Bearish. With the stock currently trading at Rs 1,057.95, Premier Energies has demonstrated notable financial improvements and growth in recent periods.

data.title: Sensex down 300 pts, Nifty below 25,000 as IT, bank stocks fall; small, midcaps outperform
data.description:
Laggards on the index included Bharti Airtel, IndusInd Bank, SBI, HCL Tech, and Infosys.

Benchmark indices Nifty and Sensex took a breather on Thursday, ending a two-day winning streak as investors locked in profits after Wednesday’s sharp rally. However, the action stayed alive in the broader market, with mid- and small-cap indices extending their winning streak to a fifth straight session, once again stealing the spotlight from the frontline peers.

At about 9:45 am, the Sensex was down 267.33 points or 0.32 percent at 82,263.41, and the Nifty was down 66.40 points or 0.26 percent at 24,995.70. About 2128 shares advanced, 854 shares declined, and 130 shares unchanged.

Yesterday, after a choppy start, bulls made a powerful comeback on Dalal Street, propelling the Nifty past the 25,000 mark for the first time since October 2024, ending a 141-session wait. Furthermore, in Doha, US President Donald Trump said India had offered a zero-tariff trade deal to the United States, boosting the sentiment further.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

"With results mostly behind us, the element of uncertainty has reduced. Furthermore, easing border tensions and a possible US-India trade agreement have created a calm environment for investors," Rajesh Palviya, Head of Technical Research at Axis Securities, said in a conversation with Moneycontrol. He added that FIIs turning net buyers and cooling inflation, which gives the RBI more room to cut rates, is also a big positive for the markets.

Sectorally, Nifty Energy and Consumer Durables rose 0.65 percent and 0.55 percent, respectively, while Realty, Oil & Gas, Metal, Auto, and PSU Bank indices opened with marginal gains. On the flip side, Nifty IT slipped 0.31 percent, Pharma dropped 0.29 percent, and the Bank indices edged lower by around 0.2 percent each. The India VIX, a gauge of market volatility, eased 2 percent to 16.5.

Also read: IndusInd Bank shares fall 4% as brokerages slash earnings forecast on fresh accounting issues

IndusInd Bank shares crashed 4 percent in the morning as brokerages turned more cautious after the lender disclosed two new accounting lapses. Analysts have not only downgraded the stock but also flagged sharp cuts to earnings estimates for FY25 through FY27. CLSA downgraded the stock to 'hold' from 'buy' and slashed the target price to Rs 780. The brokerage has slashed its profit after tax (PAT) forecast for IndusInd Bank by 22 percent for FY25.

Airtel shares were down 3 percent after Singapore-based Singtel gears up to pare its stake in the telecom giant through its investment arm, Pastel. As per CNBC TV-18, Pastel is expected to offload around 0.8 percent of its holding in Bharti Airtel via the transaction. The floor price has been set at Rs 1,800 per share—implying a discount of 3.6 percent to the stock’s closing price on Thursday.

Shares of LIC Housing Finance Ltd fell 2 percent on Thursday (May 15) despite reporting a 25.4 percent year-on-year (YoY) jump in net profit at Rs 1,368 crore for the fourth quarter that ended March 31, 2025. In the corresponding quarter of the previous fiscal, LIC Housing Finance posted a net profit of Rs 1,090.8 crore.

Read more: JSW Infrastructure promoter entity launches Rs 1,200 crore block deal 

"Immediate support is placed at 24,850–24,700, while resistance is seen at 25,100 and 25,235. A decisive breakout above the 25,235 level could drive the index higher toward the 25,500–25,743 zone. Traders are advised to adopt a "buy on dips" strategy with strict risk management and avoid taking large overnight positions due to ongoing global uncertainties," Hardik Matalia, Derivative Analyst at Choice Broking said.

The Bank Nifty surged 554 points and formed a bullish candle, signalling continued buying interest. A sustained move above the key resistance level of 55,500 could lead to further upside. Immediate support is located at 55,000 and 54,720, while resistance on the higher side is seen at 55,700 and 56,000. A decisive close above this range may trigger a rally toward the 56,500 mark, he added.

Bharat Electronics, NTPC, Eicher Motors, Eternal and Adani Enterprises were the top gainers on the Nifty. Laggards included Bharti Airtel, IndusInd Bank, SBI, HCL Tech, and Infosys.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to consult certified experts before making any investment decisions.
data.author: MoneyControl
